content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

content.content:
1→# srcref - Dynamic Line-Specific GitHub Permalinks
     2→
     3→[![](https://jitpack.io/v/pambrose/srcref.svg)](https://jitpack.io/#pambrose/srcref)
     4→![GitHub release (latest by date)](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/pambrose/srcref)
     5→[![Kotlin version](https://img.shields.io/badge/kotlin-2.2.0-red?logo=kotlin)](http://kotlinlang.org)
     6→[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/pambrose/srcref)](https://github.com/pambrose/srcref/blob/master/LICENSE.md)
     7→
     8→## Overview
     9→
    10→It is problematic to embed line-specific GitHub permalinks in documentation because
    11→changes to the target file can invalidate the permalink line references.
    12→
    13→**srcref** solves this problem by using regex patterns to dynamically locate code sections,
    14→generating GitHub permalinks that remain valid even when files are modified.
    15→
    16→**Key Benefits:**
    17→
    18→- 🔗 **Dynamic permalinks** that survive code changes
    19→- 🎯 **Regex-based targeting** for precise code section selection
    20→- ⚡ **Fast performance** with intelligent caching
    21→- 🌐 **Public service** available at [www.srcref.com](https://www.srcref.com)
    22→- 🔧 **Self-hostable** for private repositories and custom deployments
    23→- 📚 **Programmatic API** for automation and tooling integration
    24→
    25→## Quick Start
    26→
    27→### Using the Web Interface
    28→
    29→1. Visit [www.srcref.com](https://www.srcref.com)
    30→2. Fill in your GitHub repository details and regex patterns
    31→3. Generate and copy your dynamic permalink
    32→4. Use the permalink in your documentation
    33→
    34→### Using the API
    35→
    36→```kotlin
    37→val permalink = Api.srcrefUrl(
    38→    account = "pambrose",
    39→    repo = "srcref",
    40→    path = "src/main/kotlin/Main.kt",
    41→    beginRegex = "fun main",
    42→    endRegex = "embeddedServer"
    43→)
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Usage
    47→
    48→1) Enter the _srcref_ information in the form [here](https://www.srcref.com).
    49→   The `regex` values use [this syntax](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/util/regex/Pattern.html).
    50→   Remember to protect regex characters like `()`, `[]` and `{}` by prefixing them with a `\`.
    51→   Use [regex101.com](https://regex101.com) to assist in creating regex values.
    52→2) If the _End_ _Regex_ field is empty, the _srcref_ URL will highlight a single line on GitHub.
    53→3) Click the "Generate URL" button to generate the _srcref_ URL.
    54→4) Click the "View GitHub Permalink" button to verify the line-specific GitHub permalink.
    55→5) Click the "Copy URL" button to copy the _srcref_ URL to your clipboard.
    56→6) Paste the _srcref_ URL into your documentation.
    57→
    58→## Example
    59→
    60→To highlight all the lines from the first occurrence
    61→of `install(CallLogging)` to 3 lines beyond the first occurrence of `install(Compression)` in
    62→[src/main/kotlin/Main.kt](https://github.com/pambrose/srcref/blob/master/src/main/kotlin/com/pambrose/srcref/Main.kt),
    63→create a _srcref_ URL using
    64→[these values](https://www.srcref.com/edit?account=pambrose&repo=srcref&branch=master&path=src%2Fmain%2Fkotlin%2Fcom%2Fpambrose%2Fsrcref%2FMain.kt&bregex=install%5C%28CallLogging%5C%29&boccur=1&boffset=0&btopd=true&eregex=install%5C%28Compression%5C%29&eoccur=1&eoffset=3&etopd=false)
    65→.
    66→
    67→The corresponding _srcref_ URL generates this
    68→[GitHub permalink](https://www.srcref.com/github?account=pambrose&repo=srcref&branch=master&path=src%2Fmain%2Fkotlin%2Fcom%2Fpambrose%2Fsrcref%2FMain.kt&bregex=install%5C%28CallLogging%5C%29&boccur=1&boffset=0&btopd=true&eregex=install%5C%28Compression%5C%29&eoccur=1&eoffset=3&etopd=false)
    69→.
    70→
    71→## Query Parameters
    72→
    73→| Parameter | Default  | Required | Description                                                |
    74→|-----------|----------|----------|------------------------------------------------------------|
    75→| _account_ |          | Yes      | GitHub account or organization name                        |
    76→| _repo_    |          | Yes      | Repo name                                                  |
    77→| _branch_  | "master" | Yes      | Branch name                                                |
    78→| _path_    |          | Yes      | File path in repo                                          |
    79→| _bregex_  |          | Yes      | The regex used to determine the beginning match            |
    80→| _boccur_  | 1        | Yes      | The number of matches for the beginning match              |
    81→| _boffset_ | 0        | Yes      | The number of lines above or below the beginning match     |
    82→| _btopd_   | true     | Yes      | The direction to evaluate the file for the beginning match |
    83→| _eregex_  |          | No       | The regex used to determine the ending match               |
    84→| _eoccur_  | 1        | No       | The number of matches for the ending match                 |
    85→| _eoffset_ | 0        | No       | The number of lines above or below the ending match        |
    86→| _etopd_   | true     | No       | The direction to evaluate the file for the ending match    |
    87→
    88→## Editing a _srcref_ URL
    89→
    90→Add `&edit` to a _srcref_ URL to edit it.
    91→
    92→## Programmatic Usage
    93→
    94→_srcref_ URLs can be generated programmatically with the `srcrefUrl()` call. An example can be seen
    95→[here](https://www.srcref.com/github?account=kslides&repo=kslides&branch=master&path=kslides-core%2Fsrc%2Fmain%2Fkotlin%2Fcom%2Fkslides%2FPresentation.kt&bregex=srcrefUrl%5C%28&boccur=1&boffset=0&btopd=true&eregex=escapeHtml4+%3D+true&eoccur=1&eoffset=1&etopd=true)
    96→.
    97→
    98→<details open>
    99→<summary>Gradle:</summary>
   100→
   101→```groovy
   102→allprojects {
   103→   repositories {
   104→      maven { url 'https://jitpack.io' }
   105→   }
   106→}
   107→```
   108→
   109→```groovy
   110→dependencies {
   111→  implementation 'com.github.pambrose:srcref:1.9.8'
   112→}
   113→```
   114→
   115→</details>
   116→
   117→
   118→<details>
   119→<summary>Maven:</summary>
   120→
   121→```xml
   122→
   123→<repositories>
   124→   <repository>
   125→      <id>jitpack.io</id>
   126→      <url>https://jitpack.io</url>
   127→   </repository>
   128→</repositories>
   129→```
   130→
   131→```xml
   132→
   133→<dependency>
   134→   <groupId>com.github.pambrose</groupId>
   135→   <artifactId>srcref</artifactId>
   136→  <version>1.9.8</version>
   137→</dependency>
   138→```
   139→
   140→</details>
   141→
   142→## How It Works
   143→
   144→srcref uses regex patterns to dynamically locate code sections in GitHub repositories:
   145→
   146→1. **Pattern Matching**: Your regex patterns are applied to the target file
   147→2. **Line Calculation**: The service finds the matching lines and applies any offsets
   148→3. **Permalink Generation**: A standard GitHub permalink is generated using the calculated line numbers
   149→4. **Intelligent Caching**: Files are cached with ETags to minimize GitHub API calls
   150→
   151→## Common Use Cases
   152→
   153→### Documentation
   154→
   155→Reference specific code sections in:
   156→
   157→- README files
   158→- Technical documentation
   159→- API documentation
   160→- Tutorial guides
   161→
   162→### Code Reviews
   163→
   164→- Link to specific problematic code sections
   165→- Reference implementations during discussions
   166→- Share code examples that stay current
   167→
   168→### Issue Tracking
   169→
   170→- Link to relevant code when reporting bugs
   171→- Reference specific lines in feature requests
   172→- Maintain valid links in long-lived issues
   173→
   174→## Regex Examples
   175→
   176→### Basic Patterns
   177→
   178→```
   179→// Match function declarations
   180→fun\s+\w+\(
   181→
   182→// Match class definitions
   183→class\s+\w+
   184→
   185→// Match import statements
   186→import\s+[\w.]+
   187→
   188→// Match specific method calls
   189→\.methodName\(
   190→```
   191→
   192→### Advanced Patterns
   193→
   194→```
   195→// Match multi-line constructs
   196→try\s*\{     // Begin regex
   197→}\s*catch   // End regex
   198→
   199→// Match configuration blocks
   200→install\([\w]+\)     // Begin regex
   201→}                    // End regex (with offset)
   202→
   203→// Match between comments
   204→//\s*START.*          // Begin regex
   205→//\s*END.*            // End regex
   206→```
   207→
   208→## Deployment Options
   209→
   210→### Option 1: Local Development
   211→
   212→```bash
   213→# Clone the repository
   214→git clone https://github.com/pambrose/srcref.git
   215→cd srcref
   216→
   217→# Run locally
   218→./gradlew build
   219→java -jar build/libs/srcref.jar
   220→```
   221→
   222→### Option 2: Docker Deployment
   223→
   224→```bash
   225→# Build Docker image
   226→docker build -t srcref .
   227→
   228→# Run with environment variables
   229→docker run -p 8080:8080 \
   230→  -e PORT=8080 \
   231→  -e PREFIX=https://your-domain.com \
   232→  srcref
   233→```
   234→
   235→### Option 3: Heroku Deployment
   236→
   237→```bash
   238→# Deploy to Heroku
   239→heroku create your-srcref-app
   240→git push heroku master
   241→```
   242→
   243→### Environment Variables
   244→
   245→| Variable         | Default                  | Description                                               |
   246→|------------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
   247→| `PORT`           | 8080                     | HTTP port to listen on                                    |
   248→| `PREFIX`         | "https://www.srcref.com" | Prefix for generated URLs                                 |
   249→| `MAX_LENGTH`     | 5MB                      | Maximum allowed file size                                 |
   250→| `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` | 2048                     | Maximum cache size before evictions                       |
   251→| `DEFAULT_BRANCH` | "master"                 | Default branch name to use if branch parameter is missing |
   252→
   253→## API Reference
   254→
   255→### `srcrefUrl()` Function
   256→
   257→```kotlin
   258→fun srcrefUrl(
   259→  account: String,              // GitHub username or organization
   260→  repo: String,                 // Repository name
   261→  path: String,                 // File path in repository
   262→  beginRegex: String,           // Regex to find start position
   263→  beginOccurrence: Int = 1,     // Which occurrence to use (1st, 2nd, etc.)
   264→  beginOffset: Int = 0,         // Lines to offset from match
   265→  beginTopDown: Boolean = true, // Search direction
   266→  endRegex: String = "",        // Optional: regex to find end position
   267→  endOccurrence: Int = 1,       // Which end occurrence to use
   268→  endOffset: Int = 0,           // Lines to offset from end match
   269→  endTopDown: Boolean = true,   // End search direction
   270→  prefix: String = "https://www.srcref.com", // Base URL
   271→  branch: String = "master",    // Git branch
   272→  escapeHtml4: Boolean = false  // HTML escape the output
   273→): String
   274→```
   275→
   276→### URL Endpoints
   277→
   278→| Endpoint       | Purpose                       | Parameters                    |
   279→|----------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
   280→| `/edit`        | Main form interface           | All query parameters          |
   281→| `/github`      | Generate GitHub permalink     | All query parameters          |
   282→| `/github?edit` | Edit existing srcref URL      | All query parameters + `edit` |
   283→| `/problem`     | Display error information     | `msg` + original parameters   |
   284→| `/what`        | Information about srcref      | None                          |
   285→| `/cache`       | Cache status and statistics   | None                          |
   286→| `/version`     | Version and build information | None                          |
   287→| `/ping`        | Health check endpoint         | None                          |
   288→
   289→## Error Handling
   290→
   291→srcref provides detailed error messages for common issues:
   292→
   293→- **Invalid regex patterns**: Syntax errors with suggestions
   294→- **Pattern not found**: Clear indication when regex doesn't match
   295→- **File too large**: Size limit exceeded warnings
   296→- **Network errors**: GitHub API connectivity issues
   297→- **Invalid parameters**: Missing or malformed inputs
   298→
   299→## Performance & Caching
   300→
   301→- **HTTP Caching**: Uses ETags to avoid unnecessary GitHub API calls
   302→- **Memory Caching**: Intelligent LRU cache with configurable size limits
   303→- **Request Optimization**: Batched operations and connection reuse
   304→- **Background Cleanup**: Automatic cache maintenance
   305→
   306→## Contributing
   307→
   308→1. Fork the repository
   309→2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)
   310→3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`)
   311→4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`)
   312→5. Open a Pull Request
   313→
   314→### Development Setup
   315→
   316→```bash
   317→# Clone repository
   318→git clone https://github.com/pambrose/srcref.git
   319→cd srcref
   320→
   321→# Build project
   322→./gradlew build
   323→
   324→# Run tests
   325→./gradlew test
   326→
   327→# Start development server
   328→./gradlew run
   329→```
   330→
   331→### Building
   332→
   333→```bash
   334→# Create executable JAR
   335→make uberjar
   336→
   337→# Build Docker image
   338→make build-docker
   339→
   340→# Run all checks
   341→make tests
   342→```
   343→
   344→## License
   345→
   346→This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md) file for details.
   347→
   348→## Acknowledgments
   349→
   350→- Built with [Ktor](https://ktor.io/) for HTTP server functionality
   351→- Uses [kotlinx.html](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.html) for type-safe HTML generation
   352→
   353→

content.content:
1→package com.pambrose.srcref
     2→
     3→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.Version
     4→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.Version.Companion.versionDesc
     5→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.getBanner
     6→import com.github.pambrose.srcref.srcref.BuildConfig
     7→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.PING
     8→import com.pambrose.srcref.Main.excludedEndpoints
     9→import com.pambrose.srcref.Routes.configureRoutes
    10→import io.github.oshai.kotlinlogging.KotlinLogging
    11→import io.ktor.http.HttpStatusCode.Companion.NotFound
    12→import io.ktor.server.application.Application
    13→import io.ktor.server.application.install
    14→import io.ktor.server.cio.CIO
    15→import io.ktor.server.engine.embeddedServer
    16→import io.ktor.server.plugins.calllogging.CallLogging
    17→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.Compression
    18→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.deflate
    19→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.gzip
    20→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.minimumSize
    21→import io.ktor.server.plugins.defaultheaders.DefaultHeaders
    22→import io.ktor.server.plugins.statuspages.StatusPages
    23→import io.ktor.server.request.path
    24→import io.ktor.server.response.respondText
    25→import org.slf4j.event.Level
    26→
    27→@Version(
    28→  version = BuildConfig.VERSION,
    29→  releaseDate = BuildConfig.RELEASE_DATE,
    30→  buildTime = BuildConfig.BUILD_TIME,
    31→)
    32→object Main {
    33→  internal val logger = KotlinLogging.logger {}
    34→  internal val excludedEndpoints = listOf("/${PING.path}")
    35→
    36→  @JvmStatic
    37→  fun main(args: Array<String>) {
    38→    logger.apply {
    39→      info { getBanner("banners/srcref.banner", this) }
    40→      info { Main::class.versionDesc() }
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    embeddedServer(
    44→      factory = CIO,
    45→      port = System.getenv("PORT")?.toInt() ?: 8080,
    46→      module = Application::module,
    47→    ).start(wait = true)
    48→  }
    49→}
    50→
    51→fun String.startsWithList(prefixes: Iterable<String>) = prefixes.any { startsWith(it) }
    52→
    53→fun Application.module() {
    54→  install(CallLogging) {
    55→    level = Level.INFO
    56→    filter { call ->
    57→      call.request.path().run {
    58→        !startsWithList(excludedEndpoints) &&
    59→          !endsWith(".php") &&
    60→          !endsWith("error.log") &&
    61→          !endsWith("error.txt")
    62→      }
    63→    }
    64→    format { call ->
    65→      val path = call.request.path()
    66→      val userAgent = call.request.headers["User-Agent"]
    67→      "[$userAgent] $path"
    68→    }
    69→  }
    70→  install(DefaultHeaders)
    71→  install(StatusPages) {
    72→    status(NotFound) { call, status ->
    73→      val filename = call.request.path()
    74→      val msg = "Page not found: $filename"
    75→      call.respondText(text = msg, status = status)
    76→    }
    77→  }
    78→  install(Compression) {
    79→    gzip { priority = 1.0 }
    80→    deflate {
    81→      priority = 10.0
    82→      minimumSize(1024)
    83→    }
    84→  }
    85→  configureRoutes()
    86→}
    87→

     2→
     3→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.*
     4→import com.pambrose.srcref.Urls.srcrefToGithubUrl
     5→
     6→@Suppress("unused")
     7→object Api {
     8→  fun srcrefUrl(
     9→    account: String,
    10→    repo: String,
    11→    path: String,
    12→    beginRegex: String,
    13→    beginOccurrence: Int = 1,
    14→    beginOffset: Int = 0,
    15→    beginTopDown: Boolean = true,
    16→    endRegex: String = "",
    17→    endOccurrence: Int = 1,
    18→    endOffset: Int = 0,
    19→    endTopDown: Boolean = true,
    20→    prefix: String = "https://www.srcref.com",
    21→    branch: String = "master",
    22→    escapeHtml4: Boolean = false,
    23→  ): String =
    24→    srcrefToGithubUrl(
    25→      mapOf(
    26→        ACCOUNT.arg to account,
    27→        REPO.arg to repo,
    28→        BRANCH.arg to branch,
    29→        PATH.arg to path,
    30→        BEGIN_REGEX.arg to beginRegex,
    31→        BEGIN_OCCURRENCE.arg to beginOccurrence.toString(),
    32→        BEGIN_OFFSET.arg to beginOffset.toString(),
    33→        BEGIN_TOPDOWN.arg to beginTopDown.toString(),
    34→        END_REGEX.arg to endRegex,
    35→        END_OCCURRENCE.arg to endOccurrence.toString(),
    36→        END_OFFSET.arg to endOffset.toString(),
    37→        END_TOPDOWN.arg to endTopDown.toString(),
    38→      ),
    39→      escapeHtml4,
    40→      prefix,
    41→    )

1→package com.pambrose.srcref
     2→
     3→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.encode
     4→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.isNotNull
     5→import com.pambrose.srcref.ContentCache.Companion.fetchContent
     6→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.GITHUB
     7→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.PROBLEM
     8→import com.pambrose.srcref.Main.logger
     9→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.ACCOUNT
    10→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_OCCURRENCE
    11→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_OFFSET
    12→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_REGEX
    13→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_TOPDOWN
    14→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BRANCH
    15→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_OCCURRENCE
    16→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_OFFSET
    17→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_REGEX
    18→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_TOPDOWN
    19→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.PATH
    20→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.REPO
    21→import com.pambrose.srcref.pages.Common.hasValues
    22→import java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException
    23→import kotlin.time.measureTimedValue
    24→import org.apache.commons.text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml4
    25→
    26→object Urls {
    27→  internal const val MSG = "msg"
    28→  internal const val RAW_PREFIX = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com"
    29→
    30→  internal fun Map<String, String?>.toQueryParams(ignoreEndParams: Boolean) =
    31→    filter { if (ignoreEndParams) it.key !in QueryParams.optionalParams else true }
    32→      .map { (k, v) -> if (v.isNotNull()) "$k=${v.encode()}" else "" }
    33→      .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
    34→      .joinToString("&")
    35→
    36→  private fun Map<String, String?>.missingEndRegex() = this[END_REGEX.arg]?.isBlank() ?: true
    37→
    38→  internal fun srcrefToGithubUrl(
    39→    params: Map<String, String?>,
    40→    escapeHtml4: Boolean = false,
    41→    prefix: String,
    42→  ) = "$prefix/$GITHUB?${params.toQueryParams(params.missingEndRegex())}"
    43→    .let { if (escapeHtml4) escapeHtml4(it) else it }
    44→
    45→  internal inline fun String?.toInt(block: () -> String) =
    46→    try {
    47→      this?.toInt() ?: throw IllegalArgumentException(block())
    48→    } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
    49→      throw IllegalArgumentException(block())
    50→    }
    51→
    52→  // This returns an url and an error message
    53→  internal suspend fun githubRangeUrl(
    54→    params: Map<String, String?>,
    55→    prefix: String,
    56→  ): Pair<String, String> =
    57→    runCatching {
    58→      if (!params.hasValues()) {
    59→        "" to ""
    60→      } else {
    61→        val account = ACCOUNT.required(params)
    62→        val repo = REPO.required(params)
    63→        val path = PATH.required(params).let { if (it.startsWith("/")) it.substring(1) else it }
    64→        val branch = BRANCH.required(params)
    65→
    66→        val url = githubRawUrl(account, repo, path, branch)
    67→        val (lines, duration) = measureTimedValue { fetchContent(url) }
    68→        logger.info { "Read ${url.removePrefix(RAW_PREFIX)} in $duration" }
    69→
    70→        val beginOffsetStr = BEGIN_OFFSET.defaultIfNull(params)
    71→        val beginOffset = beginOffsetStr.toInt { "Invalid Begin Offset value: $beginOffsetStr" }
    72→        val beginLinenum =
    73→          calcLineNumber(
    74→            lines = lines,
    75→            pattern = BEGIN_REGEX.required(params),
    76→            occurrence = BEGIN_OCCURRENCE.defaultIfNull(params).toInt(),
    77→            offset = beginOffset,
    78→            topDown = BEGIN_TOPDOWN.defaultIfNull(params).toBoolean(),
    79→            desc = "begin",
    80→          ).also { if (it < 1) throw IllegalArgumentException("Begin line number is less than 1") }
    81→
    82→        val endLinenum =
    83→          if (END_REGEX.defaultIfNull(params).isBlank()) {
    84→            -1
    85→          } else {
    86→            val endOffsetStr = END_OFFSET.defaultIfNull(params)
    87→            val endOffset = endOffsetStr.toInt { "Invalid End Offset value: $endOffsetStr" }
    88→            calcLineNumber(
    89→              lines = lines,
    90→              pattern = END_REGEX.defaultIfNull(params),
    91→              occurrence = END_OCCURRENCE.defaultIfNull(params).toInt(),
    92→              offset = endOffset,
    93→              topDown = END_TOPDOWN.defaultIfNull(params).toBoolean(),
    94→              desc = "end",
    95→            ).also { if (it < 1) throw IllegalArgumentException("End line number is less than 1") }
    96→          }
    97→        githubSourceUrl(account, repo, branch, path, beginLinenum, endLinenum) to ""
    98→      }
    99→    }.getOrElse { e ->
   100→      val msg = "${e::class.simpleName}: ${e.message}"
   101→      logger.info { "Input problem: $msg $params" }
   102→      "$prefix/$PROBLEM?$MSG=${msg.encode()}&${params.toQueryParams(false)}" to msg
   103→    }
   104→
   105→  private fun githubSourceUrl(
   106→    username: String,
   107→    repoName: String,
   108→    branchName: String,
   109→    path: String = "",
   110→    beginLineNum: Int,
   111→    endLineNum: Int,
   112→  ): String {
   113→    val suffix = if (endLineNum > 0 && beginLineNum != endLineNum) "-L$endLineNum" else ""
   114→    return "https://github.com/$username/$repoName/blob/$branchName/$path#L$beginLineNum$suffix"
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  private fun githubRawUrl(
   118→    username: String,
   119→    repoName: String,
   120→    path: String = "",
   121→    branchName: String,
   122→  ) = "$RAW_PREFIX/$username/$repoName/$branchName/$path"
   123→
   124→  internal fun calcLineNumber(
   125→    lines: List<String>,
   126→    pattern: String,
   127→    occurrence: Int,
   128→    offset: Int,
   129→    topDown: Boolean,
   130→    desc: String = "",
   131→  ): Int =
   132→    runCatching {
   133→      val regex = Regex(pattern)
   134→      (
   135→        (if (topDown) lines else lines.asReversed())
   136→          .asSequence()
   137→          .mapIndexed { index, s -> (if (topDown) index else (lines.size - index - 1)) to s.contains(regex) }
   138→          .filter { it.second }
   139→          .drop(occurrence - 1)
   140→          .first().first
   141→        ) + offset + 1
   142→    }.getOrElse { e ->
   143→      throw when (e) {
   144→        is PatternSyntaxException -> {
   145→          IllegalArgumentException("Invalid regex:\"${pattern}\" - ${e.message}")
   146→        }
   147→
   148→        is NoSuchElementException -> {
   149→          IllegalArgumentException("Required matches ($occurrence) not found for $desc regex: \"$pattern\"")
   150→        }
   151→
   152→        else -> {
   153→          logger.error { "Error in calcLineNumber(): ${e::class.simpleName}: ${e.message}" }
   154→          e
   155→        }
   156→      }
   157→    }
   158→}
   159→

1→package com.pambrose.srcref
     2→
     3→internal enum class QueryParams(
     4→  internal val arg: String,
     5→  private val defaultValue: String,
     6→  private val optional: Boolean,
     7→) {
     8→  ACCOUNT("account", "", false),
     9→  REPO("repo", "", false),
    10→  BRANCH("branch", System.getenv("DEFAULT_BRANCH") ?: "master", false),
    11→  PATH("path", "", false),
    12→  BEGIN_REGEX("bregex", "", false),
    13→  BEGIN_OCCURRENCE("boccur", "1", false),
    14→  BEGIN_OFFSET("boffset", "0", false),
    15→  BEGIN_TOPDOWN("btopd", "true", false),
    16→  END_REGEX("eregex", "", true),
    17→  END_OCCURRENCE("eoccur", "1", true),
    18→  END_OFFSET("eoffset", "0", true),
    19→  END_TOPDOWN("etopd", "true", true),
    20→  ;
    21→
    22→  fun defaultIfNull(params: Map<String, String?>) = params[arg] ?: defaultValue
    23→
    24→  fun defaultIfBlank(params: Map<String, String?>) = (params[arg] ?: "").let { it.ifBlank { defaultValue } }
    25→
    26→  fun required(params: Map<String, String?>) =
    27→    (params[arg] ?: "").let { it.ifBlank { throw IllegalArgumentException("Missing: $arg value") } }
    28→
    29→  companion object {
    30→    val optionalParams = entries.filter { it.optional }.map { it.arg }
    31→  }
    32→}
    33→

1→FROM alpine
     2→MAINTAINER Paul Ambrose "pambrose@mac.com"
     3→RUN apk add openjdk17-jre
     4→
     5→# Define the user to use in this instance to prevent using root that even in a container, can be a security risk.
     6→ENV APPLICATION_USER=srcref
     7→
     8→# Then add the user, create the /app folder and give permissions to our user.
     9→RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' $APPLICATION_USER
    10→RUN mkdir /app
    11→RUN chown -R $APPLICATION_USER /app
    12→
    13→# Mark this container to use the specified $APPLICATION_USER
    14→USER $APPLICATION_USER
    15→
    16→COPY ./build/libs/srcref-all.jar /app/srcref-all.jar
    17→COPY src/main/resources /app/src/main/resources
    18→
    19→# Make /app the working directory
    20→WORKDIR /app
    21→
    22→EXPOSE 8080
    23→
    24→CMD []
    25→# Launch java to execute the jar with defaults intended for containers.
    26→ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-server", "-XX:+UseContainerSupport", "-Xmx2048m", "-Dlogback.configurationFile=/app/src/main/resources/logback-srcref.xml", "-jar", "/app/srcref-all.jar"]
    27→

content.text:
Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive summary. I have thoroughly analyzed the codebase. Let me provide you with a detailed CLAUDE.md content:

## Summary of Codebase Analysis

Based on my thorough investigation of the srcref repository, here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### Project Overview
**srcref** is a Kotlin-based web service that generates dynamic GitHub permalinks using regex patterns. It solves the problem of maintaining valid line-specific links when code changes. The service is available at https://www.srcref.com and can be self-hosted.

---

## CLAUDE.md Content

```markdown
# srcref - Development Guide

## Project Overview

**srcref** is a dynamic link generator for GitHub repositories that creates line-specific permalinks using regex patterns. The service automatically adjusts links when source files change, eliminating broken documentation references.

- **Language**: Kotlin 2.3.10
- **Build System**: Gradle 9.3.1 with Kotlin DSL
- **Java Target**: JVM 17
- **License**: MIT

## Build System

### Build Configuration
- **Primary**: `build.gradle.kts` (Kotlin DSL)
- **Settings**: `settings.gradle.kts` (minimal, just defines root project name "srcref")
- **Version Catalog**: `gradle/libs.versions.toml` (dependency and plugin versions)
- **Gradle Wrapper**: `gradle-wrapper.properties` (version 9.3.1)

### Dependency Management

**Key Dependencies:**
- **Ktor**: 3.4.0 (HTTP server/client framework)
  - Server (CIO), HTML builder, client components
- **Kotlin Coroutines**: 1.10.2
- **Dropwizard Metrics**: 4.2.38 (JVM metrics)
- **Common Utils**: 2.5.3 (custom library for utilities and Ktor extensions)
- **Logback**: 1.5.29 (logging)
- **Kotlin Logging**: 7.0.14 (SLF4J wrapper)
- **Commons Text**: 1.15.0 (text utilities)
- **Kotest**: 6.1.3 (testing framework)

**Build Plugins:**
- Kotlin JVM
- Kotlinter (code linting/formatting)
- BuildConfig (generates build metadata)
- Gradle Versions (dependency updates checker)
- Ktor Plugin

### Building the Project

```bash
# Build without tests
./gradlew build -xtest

# Build with tests
./gradlew build

# Create executable fat JAR
./gradlew buildFatJar
# Output: build/libs/srcref-all.jar

# Run tests
./gradlew test

# Run linting checks
./gradlew check

# Check for dependency updates
./gradlew dependencyUpdates

# Run locally (development)
./gradlew run
```

**Makefile shortcuts:**
```bash
make build          # Clean and build without tests
make tests          # Run all checks with rerun
make uberjar        # Create fat JAR
make run            # Start development server
make build-docker   # Build Docker image
make release        # Full release: build, docker, push to registry
make versioncheck   # Check for dependency updates
```

### Gradle Wrapper Upgrade
```bash
./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=9.3.1 --distribution-type=bin
```

## Project Structure

### Directory Layout
```
srcref/
├── src/
│   ├── main/
│   │   ├── kotlin/com/pambrose/srcref/
│   │   │   ├── Main.kt                    # Entry point, server config
│   │   │   ├── Routes.kt                  # HTTP route definitions
│   │   │   ├── Endpoints.kt               # Endpoint enum
│   │   │   ├── Api.kt                     # Public API function
│   │   │   ├── Urls.kt                    # URL generation and regex matching
│   │   │   ├── QueryParams.kt             # Query parameter definitions
│   │   │   ├── ContentCache.kt            # Content caching with ETag support
│   │   │   └── pages/
│   │   │       ├── PageTemplate.kt        # HTML template base class
│   │   │       ├── Common.kt              # Shared page utilities
│   │   │       ├── Edit.kt                # Main form page
│   │   │       ├── Error.kt               # Error display page
│   │   │       ├── Version.kt             # Version info page
│   │   │       ├── What.kt                # "What is srcref?" page
│   │   │       └── Cache.kt               # Cache stats page
│   │   └── resources/
│   │       ├── logback-srcref.xml         # Logging configuration
│   │       ├── banners/
│   │       │   └── srcref.banner          # ASCII art banner
│   │       └── public/                    # Static assets
│   │           ├── css/srcref.css
│   │           ├── js/copyUrl.js
│   │           └── images/
│   └── test/
│       └── kotlin/
│           └── FileTests.kt               # KoTest tests
├── gradle/
│   ├── libs.versions.toml                 # Dependency versions
│   └── wrapper/                           # Gradle wrapper files
├── build.gradle.kts
├── settings.gradle.kts
├── Makefile                               # Build automation
├── Dockerfile                             # Container image definition
├── README.md                              # User documentation
├── LICENSE.md                             # MIT License
├── Procfile                               # Heroku deployment config
├── system.properties                      # Java runtime version
└── jitpack.yml                            # JitPack build config
```

## Source Code Organization

### Core Architecture

**Main Components:**

1. **Main.kt** - Application Entry Point
   - Embeds Ktor CIO server on port 8080 (configurable via `PORT` env var)
   - Configures logging filters, compression, error handling
   - Installs middleware: CallLogging, DefaultHeaders, StatusPages, Compression

2. **Routes.kt** - HTTP Routing
   - Maps endpoints to handlers
   - Endpoints: `/edit`, `/github`, `/problem`, `/what`, `/cache`, `/version`, `/ping`, `/threaddump`, `/robots.txt`
   - Static resource serving from `/public`

3. **Urls.kt** - Core Logic
   - `calcLineNumber()`: Regex-based line finding algorithm
   - `githubRangeUrl()`: Generates GitHub URLs with line ranges
   - Supports top-down and bottom-up regex matching
   - Occurrence selection and line offset calculations

4. **ContentCache.kt** - Intelligent Content Caching
   - ETag-based HTTP caching to minimize GitHub API calls
   - LRU eviction when cache exceeds `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` (default: 2048)
   - Background cleanup thread (5-minute intervals)
   - File size limit: `MAX_LENGTH` (default: 5MB)
   - Tracks cache hits, age, last referenced time

5. **Api.kt** - Public Programmatic API
   - `srcrefUrl()` function for programmatic URL generation
   - Used in dependent projects

6. **Pages/** - HTML UI Components
   - **PageTemplate.kt**: Base HTML template with header, CSS, favicon
   - **Edit.kt**: Main form interface with regex input fields
   - **Error.kt**: Error message display page
   - **Version.kt**: Build info, version, release date
   - **What.kt**: Service information page
   - **Cache.kt**: Cache statistics and analytics
   - **Common.kt**: Shared utilities, URL prefix management

### Query Parameters

Enum `QueryParams` defines all URL parameters:
- `account`: GitHub username/org (required)
- `repo`: Repository name (required)
- `branch`: Branch name (default: master or `DEFAULT_BRANCH` env var)
- `path`: File path (required)
- `bregex`: Begin regex pattern (required)
- `boccur`: Begin occurrence number (default: 1)
- `boffset`: Begin line offset (default: 0)
- `btopd`: Begin top-down search (default: true)
- `eregex`: End regex pattern (optional)
- `eoccur`: End occurrence number (default: 1)
- `eoffset`: End line offset (default: 0)
- `etopd`: End top-down search (default: true)

### Key Endpoints

| Path | Purpose | Input |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/edit` | Main form interface | Query params |
| `/github` | Redirect to GitHub permalink | Query params |
| `/?edit` | Edit existing srcref URL | Query params + `edit` flag |
| `/problem` | Show error details | `msg` + original params |
| `/what` | Information page | None |
| `/cache` | Cache statistics | None |
| `/version` | Version/build info | None |
| `/ping` | Health check | None |
| `/threaddump` | JVM thread dump | None |

## Testing

### Test Framework
- **Kotest**: JUnit5-compatible testing with StringSpec DSL
- **Location**: `src/test/kotlin/FileTests.kt`

### Test Structure
```kotlin
class FileTests : StringSpec({
  "String.toIntList()" {
    // Comprehensive tests for calcLineNumber() function
    val input = listOf("aaa", "aaa", "aaa", "bbb", "bbb", "bbb", ...)
    
    calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 0, true) shouldBe 1
    calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 1, true) shouldBe 2
    // etc.
  }
})
```

### Running Tests
```bash
./gradlew test                # Run all tests
./gradlew --rerun-tasks check # Run with forcing rerun

# Test configuration (build.gradle.kts)
tasks.test {
  useJUnitPlatform()
  testLogging {
    events("passed", "skipped", "failed", "standardOut", "standardError")
    exceptionFormat = TestExceptionFormat.FULL
    showStandardStreams = true
  }
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `PORT` | 8080 | HTTP server port |
| `PREFIX` | https://www.srcref.com | URL prefix for generated links |
| `MAX_LENGTH` | 5MB | Maximum file size to process |
| `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` | 2048 | Maximum number of cached files |
| `DEFAULT_BRANCH` | master | Default branch for GitHub repos |

### Runtime Configuration

**Logging**: `src/main/resources/logback-srcref.xml`
- Uses SLF4J + Logback
- Info level for request logging

**Code Style**: `.editorconfig`
- UTF-8 encoding
- LF line endings
- 2-space indentation
- 120 character line length limit

## Linting & Code Quality

### Kotlinter Configuration
```kotlin
kotlinter {
  reporters = arrayOf("checkstyle", "plain")
}
```

**Disabled Checks** (in .editorconfig):
- No wildcard imports
- String template indent
- General indentation
- Chain method continuation

### Running Linting
```bash
./gradlew lintKotlin    # Check
./gradlew formatKotlin  # Fix
```

## Deployment

### Docker
**Dockerfile Strategy:**
- Base: Alpine Linux with OpenJDK 17 JRE
- Runs as non-root user `srcref` (security best practice)
- Expects fat JAR at `build/libs/srcref-all.jar`
- Mounts resources directory
- Exposes port 8080
- Configured for 2GB heap

**Building & Running:**
```bash
./gradlew buildFatJar
docker build -t srcref:latest .
docker run -p 8080:8080 -e PORT=8080 srcref:latest
```

### Heroku Deployment
- **Procfile**: Starts Java with port from `$PORT` env var
- **system.properties**: Specifies Java 17 runtime
- **JitPack Support**: `jitpack.yml` configures OpenJDK 17 for library publishing

### JitPack Library Publishing
```bash
./gradlew clean build -xtest
./gradlew publishToMavenLocal
```

Build configuration:
- `groupId`: com.github.pambrose.srcref
- `artifactId`: srcref
- Version: Synced from `build.gradle.kts`

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
```bash
# Clone and setup
git clone https://github.com/pambrose/srcref.git
cd srcref

# Run development server
./gradlew run
# Visit http://localhost:8080/edit

# Make changes, test locally
./gradlew test

# Run linting
./gradlew check

# View dependency updates
./gradlew dependencyUpdates
```

### Version Management
Version is centrally managed in `build.gradle.kts`:
```kotlin
version = "1.9.8"
```

**Important**: Update version in:
- `build.gradle.kts`
- `README.md`
- `Makefile` (VERSION variable)

### Publishing Releases
```bash
make release  # Full workflow: clean, build, docker, push
```

Steps:
1. Build fat JAR: `./gradlew buildFatJar`
2. Build Docker image
3. Push multiarch image (amd64, arm64) to Docker Hub

## Key Patterns & Design Decisions

### Regex-Based URL Matching
- Uses Java's `java.util.regex.Pattern` (full regex support)
- Supports both top-down and bottom-up searching
- Allows multiple matches with occurrence selection
- Validates regex syntax and provides helpful error messages

### Content Caching Strategy
- **ETag-based**: Minimizes bandwidth and GitHub API calls
- **LRU Eviction**: Background thread removes least-recently-used entries
- **Memory Efficient**: Configurable size limits
- **Thread-safe**: ConcurrentHashMap for concurrent access

### Type-Safe HTML Generation
- Uses **kotlinx.html** for DSL-based HTML building
- Template system with composable Placeholder components
- Type-safe attributes and elements

### Ktor Plugin Integration
- CIO (Coroutine I/O) engine for high performance
- Kotlin coroutines for async operations
- Compression (gzip, deflate)
- Call logging with user-agent tracking
- Status page customization

### Dependency Injection Pattern
- Companion objects for singletons (ContentCache, ThreadDump)
- Lazy initialization where appropriate
- Clean separation of concerns

## Special Features

### Intelligent Filtering
- Filters out bot/crawler requests
- Ignores `.php`, `error.log`, `error.txt` requests
- Excludes health checks from logging

### Metrics & Monitoring
- Dropwizard metrics for JVM stats
- Thread dump endpoint for diagnostics
- Cache statistics endpoint
- Health check endpoint (`/ping`)

### Error Handling
- Detailed error messages for:
  - Invalid regex patterns
  - Pattern not found
  - File size limits exceeded
  - Missing required parameters
- User-friendly error display pages

## Documentation

### User Documentation
- **README.md**: Comprehensive usage guide with examples
- **What is srcref page**: Information about the service
- **Query parameters reference**: Detailed parameter documentation
- **Regex examples**: Common and advanced patterns

### API Documentation
- `srcrefUrl()` Kotlin function documented in README
- Example usage in README
- Maven/Gradle dependency instructions

## Recent Changes (from git history)

- Upgraded Gradle to 9.3.1
- Updated Kotlin to 2.3.10
- Enhanced dependency version rejection logic (non-stable versions)
- Refactored PageTemplate for better formatting
- Migrated to template-based HTML response system
- Updated library versions (utils 2.5.3, ktor 3.4.0, etc.)

## Running Individual Commands

```bash
# Quick test run
./gradlew test --info

# Full validation
./gradlew clean build check

# Format code
./gradlew formatKotlin

# Check dependencies
./gradlew dependencyUpdates -Drevision=release

# Run application with custom port
PPORT=3000 ./gradlew run

# Create distribution
./gradlew installDist

# Stop Gradle daemon
./gradlew --stop
```

## Notes for Contributors

1. **Code Style**: Run `./gradlew formatKotlin` before committing
2. **Testing**: Always run `./gradlew test` to ensure tests pass
3. **Versions**: Centralize in `libs.versions.toml` and `build.gradle.kts`
4. **Documentation**: Update README.md for user-facing changes
5. **Logging**: Use kotlin-logging with appropriate levels
6. **Kotlin Opt-ins**: Experimental features are opt-in via source set config
```

---

## Key Findings Summary

1. **Build System**: Modern Gradle with Kotlin DSL, centralized dependency management via version catalog
2. **Architecture**: Clean separation - routes → pages, core logic in Urls.kt, intelligent caching in ContentCache
3. **Technology Stack**: Ktor for HTTP, Kotlin coroutines, Dropwizard metrics, kotlinx.html for UI
4. **Testing**: Kotest with comprehensive line-number calculation tests
5. **Deployment**: Docker-ready, Heroku-compatible, JitPack library publishing
6. **Development**: Well-structured with clear concerns, environment variable configuration, comprehensive logging
7. **Documentation**: Good README, in-code documentation, clear API surface

The codebase follows Kotlin best practices with coroutines, lazy initialization, and functional programming patterns. It's well-designed for scalability with intelligent caching and resource management.

**Makefile shortcuts:**

```

## Source Code Organization

### Core Architecture

**Main Components:**

1. **Main.kt** - Application Entry Point
   - Embeds Ktor CIO server on port 8080 (configurable via `PORT` env var)
   - Configures logging filters, compression, error handling
   - Installs middleware: CallLogging, DefaultHeaders, StatusPages, Compression

2. **Routes.kt** - HTTP Routing
   - Maps endpoints to handlers
   - Endpoints: `/edit`, `/github`, `/problem`, `/what`, `/cache`, `/version`, `/ping`, `/threaddump`, `/robots.txt`
   - Static resource serving from `/public`

3. **Urls.kt** - Core Logic
   - `calcLineNumber()`: Regex-based line finding algorithm
   - `githubRangeUrl()`: Generates GitHub URLs with line ranges
   - Supports top-down and bottom-up regex matching
   - Occurrence selection and line offset calculations

4. **ContentCache.kt** - Intelligent Content Caching
   - ETag-based HTTP caching to minimize GitHub API calls
   - LRU eviction when cache exceeds `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` (default: 2048)
   - Background cleanup thread (5-minute intervals)
   - File size limit: `MAX_LENGTH` (default: 5MB)
   - Tracks cache hits, age, last referenced time

5. **Api.kt** - Public Programmatic API
   - `srcrefUrl()` function for programmatic URL generation
   - Used in dependent projects

6. **Pages/** - HTML UI Components
   - **PageTemplate.kt**: Base HTML template with header, CSS, favicon
   - **Edit.kt**: Main form interface with regex input fields
   - **Error.kt**: Error message display page
   - **Version.kt**: Build info, version, release date
   - **What.kt**: Service information page
   - **Cache.kt**: Cache statistics and analytics
   - **Common.kt**: Shared utilities, URL prefix management

### Query Parameters

Enum `QueryParams` defines all URL parameters:
- `account`: GitHub username/org (required)
- `repo`: Repository name (required)
- `branch`: Branch name (default: master or `DEFAULT_BRANCH` env var)
- `path`: File path (required)
- `bregex`: Begin regex pattern (required)
- `boccur`: Begin occurrence number (default: 1)
- `boffset`: Begin line offset (default: 0)
- `btopd`: Begin top-down search (default: true)
- `eregex`: End regex pattern (optional)
- `eoccur`: End occurrence number (default: 1)
- `eoffset`: End line offset (default: 0)
- `etopd`: End top-down search (default: true)

### Key Endpoints

| Path | Purpose | Input |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/edit` | Main form interface | Query params |
| `/github` | Redirect to GitHub permalink | Query params |
| `/?edit` | Edit existing srcref URL | Query params + `edit` flag |
| `/problem` | Show error details | `msg` + original params |
| `/what` | Information page | None |
| `/cache` | Cache statistics | None |
| `/version` | Version/build info | None |
| `/ping` | Health check | None |
| `/threaddump` | JVM thread dump | None |

## Testing

### Test Framework
- **Kotest**: JUnit5-compatible testing with StringSpec DSL
- **Location**: `src/test/kotlin/FileTests.kt`

### Test Structure
```kotlin
class FileTests : StringSpec({
  "String.toIntList()" {
    // Comprehensive tests for calcLineNumber() function
    val input = listOf("aaa", "aaa", "aaa", "bbb", "bbb", "bbb", ...)
    
    calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 0, true) shouldBe 1
    calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 1, true) shouldBe 2
    // etc.
  }
})
```

### Running Tests
```bash
./gradlew test                # Run all tests
./gradlew --rerun-tasks check # Run with forcing rerun

# Test configuration (build.gradle.kts)
tasks.test {
  useJUnitPlatform()
  testLogging {
    events("passed", "skipped", "failed", "standardOut", "standardError")
    exceptionFormat = TestExceptionFormat.FULL
    showStandardStreams = true
  }
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `PORT` | 8080 | HTTP server port |
| `PREFIX` | https://www.srcref.com | URL prefix for generated links |
| `MAX_LENGTH` | 5MB | Maximum file size to process |
| `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` | 2048 | Maximum number of cached files |
| `DEFAULT_BRANCH` | master | Default branch for GitHub repos |

### Runtime Configuration

**Logging**: `src/main/resources/logback-srcref.xml`
- Uses SLF4J + Logback
- Info level for request logging

**Code Style**: `.editorconfig`
- UTF-8 encoding
- LF line endings
- 2-space indentation
- 120 character line length limit

## Linting & Code Quality

### Kotlinter Configuration
```kotlin
kotlinter {
  reporters = arrayOf("checkstyle", "plain")
}
```

**Disabled Checks** (in .editorconfig):
- No wildcard imports
- String template indent
- General indentation
- Chain method continuation

### Running Linting
```bash
./gradlew lintKotlin    # Check
./gradlew formatKotlin  # Fix
```

## Deployment

### Docker
**Dockerfile Strategy:**
- Base: Alpine Linux with OpenJDK 17 JRE
- Runs as non-root user `srcref` (security best practice)
- Expects fat JAR at `build/libs/srcref-all.jar`
- Mounts resources directory
- Exposes port 8080
- Configured for 2GB heap

**Building & Running:**
```bash
./gradlew buildFatJar
docker build -t srcref:latest .
docker run -p 8080:8080 -e PORT=8080 srcref:latest
```

### Heroku Deployment
- **Procfile**: Starts Java with port from `$PORT` env var
- **system.properties**: Specifies Java 17 runtime
- **JitPack Support**: `jitpack.yml` configures OpenJDK 17 for library publishing

### JitPack Library Publishing
```bash
./gradlew clean build -xtest
./gradlew publishToMavenLocal
```

Build configuration:
- `groupId`: com.github.pambrose.srcref
- `artifactId`: srcref
- Version: Synced from `build.gradle.kts`

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
```bash
# Clone and setup
git clone https://github.com/pambrose/srcref.git
cd srcref

# Run development server
./gradlew run
# Visit http://localhost:8080/edit

# Make changes, test locally
./gradlew test

# Run linting
./gradlew check

# View dependency updates
./gradlew dependencyUpdates
```

### Version Management
Version is centrally managed in `build.gradle.kts`:
```kotlin
version = "1.9.8"
```

**Important**: Update version in:
- `build.gradle.kts`
- `README.md`
- `Makefile` (VERSION variable)

### Publishing Releases
```bash
make release  # Full workflow: clean, build, docker, push
```

Steps:
1. Build fat JAR: `./gradlew buildFatJar`
2. Build Docker image
3. Push multiarch image (amd64, arm64) to Docker Hub

## Key Patterns & Design Decisions

### Regex-Based URL Matching
- Uses Java's `java.util.regex.Pattern` (full regex support)
- Supports both top-down and bottom-up searching
- Allows multiple matches with occurrence selection
- Validates regex syntax and provides helpful error messages

### Content Caching Strategy
- **ETag-based**: Minimizes bandwidth and GitHub API calls
- **LRU Eviction**: Background thread removes least-recently-used entries
- **Memory Efficient**: Configurable size limits
- **Thread-safe**: ConcurrentHashMap for concurrent access

### Type-Safe HTML Generation
- Uses **kotlinx.html** for DSL-based HTML building
- Template system with composable Placeholder components
- Type-safe attributes and elements

### Ktor Plugin Integration
- CIO (Coroutine I/O) engine for high performance
- Kotlin coroutines for async operations
- Compression (gzip, deflate)
- Call logging with user-agent tracking
- Status page customization

### Dependency Injection Pattern
- Companion objects for singletons (ContentCache, ThreadDump)
- Lazy initialization where appropriate
- Clean separation of concerns

## Special Features

### Intelligent Filtering
- Filters out bot/crawler requests
- Ignores `.php`, `error.log`, `error.txt` requests
- Excludes health checks from logging

### Metrics & Monitoring
- Dropwizard metrics for JVM stats
- Thread dump endpoint for diagnostics
- Cache statistics endpoint
- Health check endpoint (`/ping`)

### Error Handling
- Detailed error messages for:
  - Invalid regex patterns
  - Pattern not found
  - File size limits exceeded
  - Missing required parameters
- User-friendly error display pages

## Documentation

### User Documentation
- **README.md**: Comprehensive usage guide with examples
- **What is srcref page**: Information about the service
- **Query parameters reference**: Detailed parameter documentation
- **Regex examples**: Common and advanced patterns

### API Documentation
- `srcrefUrl()` Kotlin function documented in README
- Example usage in README
- Maven/Gradle dependency instructions

## Recent Changes (from git history)

- Upgraded Gradle to 9.3.1
- Updated Kotlin to 2.3.10
- Enhanced dependency version rejection logic (non-stable versions)
- Refactored PageTemplate for better formatting
- Migrated to template-based HTML response system
- Updated library versions (utils 2.5.3, ktor 3.4.0, etc.)

## Running Individual Commands

```bash
# Quick test run
./gradlew test --info

# Full validation
./gradlew clean build check

# Format code
./gradlew formatKotlin

# Check dependencies
./gradlew dependencyUpdates -Drevision=release

# Run application with custom port
PPORT=3000 ./gradlew run

# Create distribution
./gradlew installDist

# Stop Gradle daemon
./gradlew --stop
```

## Notes for Contributors

1. **Code Style**: Run `./gradlew formatKotlin` before committing
2. **Testing**: Always run `./gradlew test` to ensure tests pass
3. **Versions**: Centralize in `libs.versions.toml` and `build.gradle.kts`
4. **Documentation**: Update README.md for user-facing changes
5. **Logging**: Use kotlin-logging with appropriate levels
6. **Kotlin Opt-ins**: Experimental features are opt-in via source set config
```

---

## Key Findings Summary

1. **Build System**: Modern Gradle with Kotlin DSL, centralized dependency management via version catalog
2. **Architecture**: Clean separation - routes → pages, core logic in Urls.kt, intelligent caching in ContentCache
3. **Technology Stack**: Ktor for HTTP, Kotlin coroutines, Dropwizard metrics, kotlinx.html for UI
4. **Testing**: Kotest with comprehensive line-number calculation tests
5. **Deployment**: Docker-ready, Heroku-compatible, JitPack library publishing
6. **Development**: Well-structured with clear concerns, environment variable configuration, comprehensive logging
7. **Documentation**: Good README, in-code documentation, clear API surface

The codebase follows Kotlin best practices with coroutines, lazy initialization, and functional programming patterns. It's well-designed for scalability with intelligent caching and resource management.

VERSION=1.9.8

default: versioncheck

build-all: clean stage

stop:
	./gradlew --stop

clean:
	./gradlew clean

compile: build

build:	clean
	./gradlew build -xtest

run:
	./gradlew run

tests:
	./gradlew --rerun-tasks check

uberjar:
	./gradlew buildFatJar

uber: uberjar
	java -jar build/libs/srcref-all.jar

run-docker:
	docker run --rm --env-file=docker_env_vars -p 8080:8080 pambrose/srcref:${VERSION}

build-docker:
	docker build -t pambrose/srcref:${VERSION} .

PLATFORMS := linux/amd64,linux/arm64/v8
IMAGE_NAME := pambrose/srcref

docker-push:
	# prepare multiarch
	docker buildx use buildx 2>/dev/null || docker buildx create --use --name=buildx
	docker buildx build --platform ${PLATFORMS} --push -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:latest -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:${VERSION} .

release: clean build uberjar build-docker docker-push

dist:
	./gradlew installDist

stage:
	./gradlew stage

purge:
	 heroku builds:cache:purge -a srcref --confirm srcref

versioncheck:
	./gradlew dependencyUpdates

upgrade-wrapper:
	./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=9.3.1 --distribution-type=bin

1→package com.pambrose.srcref
     2→
     3→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.Version
     4→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.Version.Companion.versionDesc
     5→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.getBanner
     6→import com.github.pambrose.srcref.srcref.BuildConfig
     7→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.PING
     8→import com.pambrose.srcref.Main.excludedEndpoints
     9→import com.pambrose.srcref.Routes.configureRoutes
    10→import io.github.oshai.kotlinlogging.KotlinLogging
    11→import io.ktor.http.HttpStatusCode.Companion.NotFound
    12→import io.ktor.server.application.Application
    13→import io.ktor.server.application.install
    14→import io.ktor.server.cio.CIO
    15→import io.ktor.server.engine.embeddedServer
    16→import io.ktor.server.plugins.calllogging.CallLogging
    17→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.Compression
    18→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.deflate
    19→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.gzip
    20→import io.ktor.server.plugins.compression.minimumSize
    21→import io.ktor.server.plugins.defaultheaders.DefaultHeaders
    22→import io.ktor.server.plugins.statuspages.StatusPages
    23→import io.ktor.server.request.path
    24→import io.ktor.server.response.respondText
    25→import org.slf4j.event.Level
    26→
    27→@Version(
    28→  version = BuildConfig.VERSION,
    29→  releaseDate = BuildConfig.RELEASE_DATE,
    30→  buildTime = BuildConfig.BUILD_TIME,
    31→)
    32→object Main {
    33→  internal val logger = KotlinLogging.logger {}
    34→  internal val excludedEndpoints = listOf("/${PING.path}")
    35→
    36→  @JvmStatic
    37→  fun main(args: Array<String>) {
    38→    logger.apply {
    39→      info { getBanner("banners/srcref.banner", this) }
    40→      info { Main::class.versionDesc() }
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    embeddedServer(
    44→      factory = CIO,
    45→      port = System.getenv("PORT")?.toInt() ?: 8080,
    46→      module = Application::module,
    47→    ).start(wait = true)
    48→  }
    49→}
    50→
    51→fun String.startsWithList(prefixes: Iterable<String>) = prefixes.any { startsWith(it) }
    52→
    53→fun Application.module() {
    54→  install(CallLogging) {
    55→    level = Level.INFO
    56→    filter { call ->
    57→      call.request.path().run {
    58→        !startsWithList(excludedEndpoints) &&
    59→          !endsWith(".php") &&
    60→          !endsWith("error.log") &&
    61→          !endsWith("error.txt")
    62→      }
    63→    }
    64→    format { call ->
    65→      val path = call.request.path()
    66→      val userAgent = call.request.headers["User-Agent"]
    67→      "[$userAgent] $path"
    68→    }
    69→  }
    70→  install(DefaultHeaders)
    71→  install(StatusPages) {
    72→    status(NotFound) { call, status ->
    73→      val filename = call.request.path()
    74→      val msg = "Page not found: $filename"
    75→      call.respondText(text = msg, status = status)
    76→    }
    77→  }
    78→  install(Compression) {
    79→    gzip { priority = 1.0 }
    80→    deflate {
    81→      priority = 10.0
    82→      minimumSize(1024)
    83→    }
    84→  }
    85→  configureRoutes()
    86→}
    87→

1→package com.pambrose.srcref
     2→
     3→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.encode
     4→import com.github.pambrose.common.util.isNotNull
     5→import com.pambrose.srcref.ContentCache.Companion.fetchContent
     6→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.GITHUB
     7→import com.pambrose.srcref.Endpoints.PROBLEM
     8→import com.pambrose.srcref.Main.logger
     9→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.ACCOUNT
    10→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_OCCURRENCE
    11→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_OFFSET
    12→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_REGEX
    13→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BEGIN_TOPDOWN
    14→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.BRANCH
    15→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_OCCURRENCE
    16→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_OFFSET
    17→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_REGEX
    18→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.END_TOPDOWN
    19→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.PATH
    20→import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.REPO
    21→import com.pambrose.srcref.pages.Common.hasValues
    22→import java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException
    23→import kotlin.time.measureTimedValue
    24→import org.apache.commons.text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml4
    25→
    26→object Urls {
    27→  internal const val MSG = "msg"
    28→  internal const val RAW_PREFIX = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com"
    29→
    30→  internal fun Map<String, String?>.toQueryParams(ignoreEndParams: Boolean) =
    31→    filter { if (ignoreEndParams) it.key !in QueryParams.optionalParams else true }
    32→      .map { (k, v) -> if (v.isNotNull()) "$k=${v.encode()}" else "" }
    33→      .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
    34→      .joinToString("&")
    35→
    36→  private fun Map<String, String?>.missingEndRegex() = this[END_REGEX.arg]?.isBlank() ?: true
    37→
    38→  internal fun srcrefToGithubUrl(
    39→    params: Map<String, String?>,
    40→    escapeHtml4: Boolean = false,
    41→    prefix: String,
    42→  ) = "$prefix/$GITHUB?${params.toQueryParams(params.missingEndRegex())}"
    43→    .let { if (escapeHtml4) escapeHtml4(it) else it }
    44→
    45→  internal inline fun String?.toInt(block: () -> String) =
    46→    try {
    47→      this?.toInt() ?: throw IllegalArgumentException(block())
    48→    } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
    49→      throw IllegalArgumentException(block())
    50→    }
    51→
    52→  // This returns an url and an error message
    53→  internal suspend fun githubRangeUrl(
    54→    params: Map<String, String?>,
    55→    prefix: String,
    56→  ): Pair<String, String> =
    57→    runCatching {
    58→      if (!params.hasValues()) {
    59→        "" to ""
    60→      } else {
    61→        val account = ACCOUNT.required(params)
    62→        val repo = REPO.required(params)
    63→        val path = PATH.required(params).let { if (it.startsWith("/")) it.substring(1) else it }
    64→        val branch = BRANCH.required(params)
    65→
    66→        val url = githubRawUrl(account, repo, path, branch)
    67→        val (lines, duration) = measureTimedValue { fetchContent(url) }
    68→        logger.info { "Read ${url.removePrefix(RAW_PREFIX)} in $duration" }
    69→
    70→        val beginOffsetStr = BEGIN_OFFSET.defaultIfNull(params)
    71→        val beginOffset = beginOffsetStr.toInt { "Invalid Begin Offset value: $beginOffsetStr" }
    72→        val beginLinenum =
    73→          calcLineNumber(
    74→            lines = lines,
    75→            pattern = BEGIN_REGEX.required(params),
    76→            occurrence = BEGIN_OCCURRENCE.defaultIfNull(params).toInt(),
    77→            offset = beginOffset,
    78→            topDown = BEGIN_TOPDOWN.defaultIfNull(params).toBoolean(),
    79→            desc = "begin",
    80→          ).also { if (it < 1) throw IllegalArgumentException("Begin line number is less than 1") }
    81→
    82→        val endLinenum =
    83→          if (END_REGEX.defaultIfNull(params).isBlank()) {
    84→            -1
    85→          } else {
    86→            val endOffsetStr = END_OFFSET.defaultIfNull(params)
    87→            val endOffset = endOffsetStr.toInt { "Invalid End Offset value: $endOffsetStr" }
    88→            calcLineNumber(
    89→              lines = lines,
    90→              pattern = END_REGEX.defaultIfNull(params),
    91→              occurrence = END_OCCURRENCE.defaultIfNull(params).toInt(),
    92→              offset = endOffset,
    93→              topDown = END_TOPDOWN.defaultIfNull(params).toBoolean(),
    94→              desc = "end",
    95→            ).also { if (it < 1) throw IllegalArgumentException("End line number is less than 1") }
    96→          }
    97→        githubSourceUrl(account, repo, branch, path, beginLinenum, endLinenum) to ""
    98→      }
    99→    }.getOrElse { e ->
   100→      val msg = "${e::class.simpleName}: ${e.message}"
   101→      logger.info { "Input problem: $msg $params" }
   102→      "$prefix/$PROBLEM?$MSG=${msg.encode()}&${params.toQueryParams(false)}" to msg
   103→    }
   104→
   105→  private fun githubSourceUrl(
   106→    username: String,
   107→    repoName: String,
   108→    branchName: String,
   109→    path: String = "",
   110→    beginLineNum: Int,
   111→    endLineNum: Int,
   112→  ): String {
   113→    val suffix = if (endLineNum > 0 && beginLineNum != endLineNum) "-L$endLineNum" else ""
   114→    return "https://github.com/$username/$repoName/blob/$branchName/$path#L$beginLineNum$suffix"
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  private fun githubRawUrl(
   118→    username: String,
   119→    repoName: String,
   120→    path: String = "",
   121→    branchName: String,
   122→  ) = "$RAW_PREFIX/$username/$repoName/$branchName/$path"
   123→
   124→  internal fun calcLineNumber(
   125→    lines: List<String>,
   126→    pattern: String,
   127→    occurrence: Int,
   128→    offset: Int,
   129→    topDown: Boolean,
   130→    desc: String = "",
   131→  ): Int =
   132→    runCatching {
   133→      val regex = Regex(pattern)
   134→      (
   135→        (if (topDown) lines else lines.asReversed())
   136→          .asSequence()
   137→          .mapIndexed { index, s -> (if (topDown) index else (lines.size - index - 1)) to s.contains(regex) }
   138→          .filter { it.second }
   139→          .drop(occurrence - 1)
   140→          .first().first
   141→        ) + offset + 1
   142→    }.getOrElse { e ->
   143→      throw when (e) {
   144→        is PatternSyntaxException -> {
   145→          IllegalArgumentException("Invalid regex:\"${pattern}\" - ${e.message}")
   146→        }
   147→
   148→        is NoSuchElementException -> {
   149→          IllegalArgumentException("Required matches ($occurrence) not found for $desc regex: \"$pattern\"")
   150→        }
   151→
   152→        else -> {
   153→          logger.error { "Error in calcLineNumber(): ${e::class.simpleName}: ${e.message}" }
   154→          e
   155→        }
   156→      }
   157→    }
   158→}
   159→

}

import com.pambrose.srcref.Urls.calcLineNumber
import io.kotest.core.spec.style.StringSpec
import io.kotest.matchers.shouldBe

class FileTests :
  StringSpec(
    {
      "String.toIntList()" {

        val input =
          """
        aaa
        aaa
        aaa
        bbb
        bbb
        bbb
        ccc
        ccc
        ccc
      """.lines()
            .filter {
              it.isNotBlank()
            }

        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 0, true) shouldBe 1
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 1, true) shouldBe 2
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 2, true) shouldBe 3

        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 0, true) shouldBe 2
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 1, true) shouldBe 3
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 2, true) shouldBe 4

        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 0, true) shouldBe 4
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 1, true) shouldBe 5
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 2, true) shouldBe 6

        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 0, true) shouldBe 5
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 1, true) shouldBe 6
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 2, true) shouldBe 7

        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 0, false) shouldBe 3
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 1, false) shouldBe 4
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 1, 2, false) shouldBe 5

        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 0, false) shouldBe 2
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 1, false) shouldBe 3
        calcLineNumber(input, "aaa", 2, 2, false) shouldBe 4

        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 0, false) shouldBe 6
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 1, false) shouldBe 7
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 1, 2, false) shouldBe 8

        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 0, false) shouldBe 5
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 1, false) shouldBe 6
        calcLineNumber(input, "bbb", 2, 2, false) shouldBe 7
      }
    },
  )

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

**srcref** is a Kotlin web service that generates dynamic GitHub permalinks using regex patterns. Instead of linking to fixed line numbers that break when code changes, srcref uses regex to find the target lines dynamically. Live at https://www.srcref.com.

## Build Commands

```bash
./gradlew build -xtest        # Build without tests
./gradlew test                 # Run tests (Kotest/JUnit5)
./gradlew check                # Full check including lint
./gradlew lintKotlin           # Lint only
./gradlew formatKotlin         # Auto-format code
./gradlew run                  # Run dev server (port 8080)
./gradlew buildFatJar          # Create fat JAR at build/libs/srcref-all.jar
./gradlew dependencyUpdates    # Check for dependency updates
```

Makefile shortcuts: `make build`, `make tests`, `make run`, `make uberjar`, `make release`.

## Architecture

**Entry point**: `Main.kt` — Embeds Ktor CIO server, installs middleware (CallLogging, Compression, StatusPages).

**Request flow**: `Main.kt` → `Routes.kt` → handler logic

Core modules in `src/main/kotlin/com/pambrose/srcref/`:

- **Routes.kt** — HTTP route definitions. Maps endpoints to handlers, reads query params from requests.
- **Urls.kt** — Core logic. `calcLineNumber()` is the key algorithm: searches file lines by regex with occurrence selection, top-down/bottom-up direction, and line offset. `githubRangeUrl()` orchestrates fetching content and building the GitHub URL.
- **ContentCache.kt** — ETag-based HTTP cache for GitHub raw file content. LRU eviction via background thread (5-min intervals). Uses `ConcurrentHashMap`. Configurable via `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` (default 2048) and `MAX_LENGTH` (default 5MB) env vars.
- **QueryParams.kt** — Enum defining all URL query parameters with defaults and validation. Begin params (`bregex`, `boccur`, `boffset`, `btopd`) are required; end params (`eregex`, `eoccur`, `eoffset`, `etopd`) are optional.
- **Endpoints.kt** — Enum mapping endpoint names to lowercase paths.
- **Api.kt** — Public `srcrefUrl()` function for programmatic use.
- **pages/** — HTML pages using kotlinx.html DSL. `PageTemplate.kt` is the base template; `Edit.kt` (main form), `Error.kt`, `Version.kt`, `What.kt`, `Cache.kt` are individual pages.

## Key Endpoints

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/edit` | Main form UI |
| `/github` | Redirect to computed GitHub permalink |
| `/github?edit` | Edit an existing srcref URL |
| `/problem` | Error display with message |
| `/ping` | Health check |

## Testing

Tests are in `src/test/kotlin/FileTests.kt` using Kotest `StringSpec`. Tests exercise `calcLineNumber()` with various combinations of pattern, occurrence, offset, and direction (top-down vs bottom-up).

```bash
./gradlew test    # Run all tests
```

## Code Style

- Kotlinter (ktlint) for linting. Run `./gradlew formatKotlin` before committing.
- Disabled ktlint rules (via `.editorconfig`): `no-wildcard-imports`, `string-template-indent`, `indent`, `chain-method-continuation`.
- 120 char line length, 2-space indentation, UTF-8, LF line endings.

## Dependencies

Managed via `gradle/libs.versions.toml`. Key frameworks: Ktor (HTTP server/client), kotlinx.html (HTML DSL), Kotest (testing), Dropwizard Metrics (JVM metrics), kotlin-logging + Logback.

## Version Management

Version is defined in `build.gradle.kts` (`version = "1.9.8"`). Must also be updated in `Makefile` (`VERSION`) and `README.md` when changing.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `PORT` | 8080 | HTTP server port |
| `PREFIX` | https://www.srcref.com | URL prefix for generated links |
| `DEFAULT_BRANCH` | master | Default GitHub branch |
| `MAX_CACHE_SIZE` | 2048 | Max cached file entries |
| `MAX_LENGTH` | 5MB | Max file size to process |

package com.pambrose.srcref

import com.pambrose.srcref.QueryParams.*
import com.pambrose.srcref.Urls.srcrefToGithubUrl

@Suppress("unused")
object Api {
  fun srcrefUrl(
    account: String,
    repo: String,
    path: String,
    beginRegex: String,
    beginOccurrence: Int = 1,
    beginOffset: Int = 0,
    beginTopDown: Boolean = true,
    endRegex: String = "",
    endOccurrence: Int = 1,
    endOffset: Int = 0,
    endTopDown: Boolean = true,
    prefix: String = "https://www.srcref.com",
    branch: String = "master",
    escapeHtml4: Boolean = false,
  ): String =
    srcrefToGithubUrl(
      mapOf(
        ACCOUNT.arg to account,
        REPO.arg to repo,
        BRANCH.arg to branch,
        PATH.arg to path,
        BEGIN_REGEX.arg to beginRegex,
        BEGIN_OCCURRENCE.arg to beginOccurrence.toString(),
        BEGIN_OFFSET.arg to beginOffset.toString(),
        BEGIN_TOPDOWN.arg to beginTopDown.toString(),
        END_REGEX.arg to endRegex,
        END_OCCURRENCE.arg to endOccurrence.toString(),
        END_OFFSET.arg to endOffset.toString(),
        END_TOPDOWN.arg to endTopDown.toString(),
      ),
      escapeHtml4,
      prefix,
    )
}
